items.title: Police arrest three people over fatal shooting at Box Hill tower

children.content: Two men from Bonnie Brook and a woman from Maryborough have been arrested over the death of a 32-year-old man in a suspected targeted shooting.

articles.title: Residents heard gunshots before body found in Melbourne apartment tower

children.content: Homicide squad detectives from Victoria Police are investigating a fatal shooting at a Box Hill apartment building.

image.altText: Forensic services arriving at a Box Hill tower.

gallery.altText: Fire trucks parked outside a tower in Box Hill

thumbnail.altText: Fire trucks parked outside a tower in Box Hill

items.title: 'Cosplay is not consent': The rules of dressing up for conventions

children.content: Cosplayers say their hobby has had an astronomical rise in popularity over the past decade. While the subculture promotes "no limits" creativity, there are still strict rules.

image.altText: miss twisted 2

items.title: St Kilda's Lance Collard found guilty of using homophobic slur against opponent

children.content: St Kilda forward Lance Collard has been found guilty of using a homophobic slur against an opponent, leaving his AFL career hanging in the balance.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: Fresh push for Murray Basin freight rail to get more produce off trucks

children.content: North-west Victorian farmers are pushing for the completion of the Murray Basin Rail Project, more than seven years after it was originally slated to be finished.

image.altText: old broad gauge rail track near Manangatang in Victoria's west

items.title: Families demand refunds for Easter carnival that left 'kids in tears'

children.content: A Melbourne council is investigating an Easter event held at the Werribee racecourse after it was criticised by attendees, who said it felt unsafe, underwhelming and poorly organised.

image.altText: A large number of children and adults search through hay, holding baskets on a sunny day.

items.title: Snoozing seal blocks road in popular coastal spot

children.content: Locals say they are not surprised the seal, described as a regular visitor, decided to stop for a snooze on the roadway.

gallery.altText: A seal sleeping on a road.

thumbnail.altText: A seal sleeping on a road.

items.title: Victoria's 2026 election to host Australia's largest trial of low-sensory voting

children.content: State election voting will be made quieter and calmer for neurodivergent voters in Victoria.

image.altText: A man in chinos and a tshirt with a lanyard leans over to cast a vote at an election

items.title: Man pleads guilty to dealing with millions of dollars from illegal tobacco

children.content: Malek Alhmedai's hire car had $4 million stashed inside, with the money linked to illegal tobacco sales.

image.altText: Boxes of cigarettes surrounded by Australian cash.

items.title: Controversial fence dividing dog owners and environmentalists removed

children.content: A fence designed to keep dogs out of a park in Melbourne's north has been removed after community opposition.

gallery.altText: The words stop the fence spray painted on a tree.

thumbnail.altText: The words stop the fence spray painted on a tree.

items.title: Racism erupts after Melbourne announces Little India plans

children.content: Social media posts about the announcement of a Little India precinct in Melbourne are flooded with racist comments, leaving some Indians with mixed feelings about the plan.

image.altText: Purva wearing a pink sari while speaking on stage at a panel

items.title: Friends, family thank the community for helping to find missing woman

children.content: The family and friends of a woman who was missing for a week in south-western Victoria have thanked the community after she was found safe and well.

image.altText: collage of four photos of a woman with black hair

items.title: Hundreds more PSOs at Melbourne stations if Liberals win state election

children.content: The Victorian opposition is promising to recruit hundreds more protective service officers if it wins government in November.

image.altText: Three protective service officers wearing masks walk down a footpath in Melbourne's CBD.

items.title: Couple forced to move home accidentally built on wrong block

children.content: A Victorian couple has been forced to move their home that was accidentally built on the wrong block.

image.altText: a man and a woman look at the camera with a serious look on their face

items.title: Police failures exposed during inquest into 50 year cold case

children.content: Ms Green was last seen alive on February 13, 1976, with the Coroner's Court of Victoria hearing the litany of policing errors following her disappearance.

items.title: Patient Aussie ousts 'idol' Moore in Bells Beach defence

children.content: Defending champion Isabella Nichols joins Molly Picklum as the last Australians standing at the Bells Beach WSL season-opener.

image.altText: Isabella Nichols sitting on a beach during a surfing competition in Hawaii.

items.title: Egg-laying hens in cages set for better living conditions under new laws

children.content: The lives of caged hens are set to improve as Victoria implements new welfare laws, but farmers say they have not been consulted.

image.altText: A hen inside a cage inside a poultry farm

items.title: 'Melbourne-like weather' predicted for SA's Gather Round

children.content: AFL fans are facing a weekend of wet weather for Gather Round as the South Australian Premier says he is "not going to rush" negotiations on the future of the event.

image.altText: people in puffer jackets under a rotunda next to posters for gather round

items.title: Early season brilliance puts Gawn on track for record All-Australian nod

children.content: Melbourne captain Max Gawn shows no sign of slowing down as an excellent first month of the season puts him front and centre of ABC Sport's rolling AFL All-Australian team.

image.altText: Max Gawn pokes his tongue out at the camera

items.title: Farmers angered by plans to turn coal mine into lake bigger than Sydney Harbour

children.content: There are plans to turn the former Hazelwood coal mine in Victoria into a lake bigger than Sydney Harbour, but farmers and environmentalists are worried about the impact on nearby waterways.

items.title: Top neuroscientist slams AFL move to back new headgear

children.content: The AFL has backed a new helmet that aims to reduce the risk of concussion, but a top neuroscientist is calling it "bad scientific practice".

image.altText: five helmets placed on a sporting field

items.title: Comedian Grace Jarvis on most bizarre encounter working in sex shop

children.content: Grace Jarvis talks about everything from her experiences with autism, ADHD, queerness, and working in a sex shop in her stand-up. Her experiences with chronic illness? Not so much.

image.altText: Grace Jarvis smiles while holding a mic and talking on stage.

items.title: Reefs at risk as warming water sees sea urchins spread further

children.content: Millions of destructive sea urchins are spreading from NSW into Victoria and Tasmania and the chair of a Senate inquiry says reefs are at risk unless there is decisive action.

image.altText: diver under water with sea urchins

items.title: Driver seriously hurt in truck crash that shut Melbourne freeway for hours

children.content: The crash shutdown outbound lanes of the Princes Freeway at Officer in Melbourne's south-east.

image.altText: An aerial photo of fire trucks, ambulances and emergency workers surrounding two crashed trucks on a freeway on a sunny day.

items.title: Police probe alleged punch that left country footy player with broken jaw

items.title: Body found on rural Victorian property during search for missing man

children.content: Police have discovered the body of a man on a property east of Ouyen in regional Victoria.

items.title: Cyclist clinging to reeds, pulled to safety in remote Snowy River rescue

children.content: The cyclist was clinging to reeds in the middle of the Snowy River when the police air wing pulled them to safety.

image.altText: Black and white image of a cyclist in a river

items.title: Tourist hotspots share how fuel crisis impacted long weekend visitors

children.content: It was a mixed weekend for many tourism businesses across the country, amid fears that rising fuel costs would keep people home. For some, it was business as usual, but fuel uncertainty has many in the sector feeling nervous about the months ahead.

image.altText: Four children sit on a rug in a grassy field, with flowers in the foreground.

items.title: SA premier wants 'certainty' beyond a one-year Gather Round deal

children.content: The South Australian premier is pushing for the state to be awarded a long-term contract for Gather Round, but says it could take weeks before a deal about the future of the footy festival is struck.

image.altText: Both men smiling in front of media microphones at a playground

items.title: Former coach says AFL 'shamed' him over $20K fine

children.content: Ex-Port Adelaide coach Ken Hinkley, who was fined for sledging in 2024, says he felt exploited by the league's later use of footage of his theatrics as a promotional tool.

image.altText: Coach celebrates with players, staff, in front of fans, at full-time of an AFL match

items.title: Mother of eight killed in Easter crash remembered as 'the light of many'

children.content: The family of a Victorian grandmother killed in a head-on crash south of  Brisbane remember her as "selfless".

image.altText: Roza Abebwa a woman smiling holding a bouquet.

items.title: Ballarat's unique approach to tackling family violence

children.content: Ballarat's approach targets causes of domestic and family violence as rates remain high.

items.title: Council workers stop collecting rubbish across Melbourne over wage dispute

children.content: Thousands of wheelie bins in several Melbourne council areas were left unemptied on Tuesday morning as workers took industrial action over pay.

image.altText: A sign on a fence saying council workers deserve fair pay.

items.title: CFA firefighters 'really angry, frustrated' at Easter break-in

children.content: CFA firefighters are "gutted" after thousands of dollars of equipment and fuel were stolen from Terip Terip station over the Easter long weekend.

items.title: Melbourne woman Eva Lasrini remembered as devoted mother of two

children.content: A Dandenong woman whose body was found south-west of Melbourne on Saturday has been remembered as a devoted mother who "deserved a life filled with safety, happiness, and peace".

gallery.altText: A woman smiles while holding a bouquet of flowers as her two adult daughters kiss her cheeks.

thumbnail.altText: A woman smiles while holding a bouquet of flowers as her two adult daughters kiss her cheeks.
